programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW

programme.title: PL STORIES
programme.desc: Andoni Iraola went from relative obscurity to leading a once relegation-threatened club to its highest ever points tally in the Premier League. programme.title: PL REWIND
programme.desc: Darren Bent is one of a select number of players to have scored a century of goals in the Premier League. The prolific striker recalls his days at Ipswich, Charlton, Spurs, Sunderland, and Aston Villa and the goals he scored on his way to joining that elite group.

We sit down with van de Ven to explore the resilience, mentality and sheer speed, on and off the pitch that have defined his ascent.

programme.title: PL STORIES
programme.desc: When Arne Slot guided Liverpool to their 20th League title in his debut season, it marked the beginning of a new era, yet echoed a familiar tradition. Explore the rich legacy of Liverpool's managerial transitions, where through decades of change, one constant endures: the club's remarkable ability to evolve and triumph, through seamless succession.

programme.title: PL REWIND
programme.desc: From the depths of League One to becoming a Premier League and European champion, Adam Lallana emerged as one of English football's standout talents of the 2010s. The midfielder recalls his most memorable matches for Southampton and Liverpool.

programme.title: PL STORIES
programme.desc: Maya Yoshida quietly became one of the Premier League's most dependable defenders during his years at Southampton, but his influence reached far beyond the pitch. In this episode, we look back at Yoshida's journey from Japan to England's top-flight, his role as a leader for club and country, and the cultural bridge he helped build along the way.
